doc: Toy 3 of 3 (V1.3 'The Toys', ADR-0022 §3 / ADR-0032): a drag-to-strum harp. One big fader is the strum bar — stream /strum_bar/in [0..1] and the `strum` op plucks a note each time the position crosses a string boundary (both directions strum). Strings are scale degrees resolved through the tonal context, so an open full-diatonic scale reads as a harp glissando and stays in key. Each of the 8 voices is a hosted `strum-harp-voice` sub-patch (plucked saw -> percussive ADSR VCA); the voicer sums them and a brightness Good Button tones the mix through a master filter. Change the key live with /harmony/root (MIDI) or reshape the scale with /harmony/s0../harmony/s6. Sweep /brightness/in [0..1] to open the plucked tone (master filter cutoff + resonance). Set the string span with /strum/octaves [1..4].

nodes.doc: PUBLIC strum bar. Identity [0,1] pass-through; its position streams to the strum op (Signal->Signal). Send /strum_bar/in [0..1] (the fader). Rests at 0.0 (bottom string) so no note fires at load.
Position crossings -> degree plucks. 8 strings over 1 octave by default; /strum/octaves widens the span.

C major, 12-TET. The strum degrees resolve through this, so the harp is always in key; change /harmony/root to transpose, /harmony/s0..s6 to reshape the scale. The Key control steps the root across one octave (C3..C4).

Hosts 8 strum-harp-voice sub-patches. Degree plucks resolve through /harmony and allocate across the voices (polyphonic, so overlapping plucks ring together — a glissando); the voicer sums their audio.
Master filter on the summed mix; cutoff + resonance are Signal inputs driven by the Brightness Good Button.

PUBLIC Good Button. The held knob value [0,1] smoothed into a CV Signal (an `m2s` is the value->signal converter), then fanned to the two ranged maps below. Send /brightness/in [0..1]. Rests at 0.6 so the plucks are bright at load.
